conditions: Obstructive sleep apnea (suspected, not formally diagnosed)
medications: Omeprazole 20 mg daily (sometimes twice daily during flare-ups)
Famotidine 20 mg as needed for breakthrough reflux
health.mental_health: High stress and intermittent insomnia tied to fear of retaliation at work; hypervigilant about phone/email privacy and has occasional panic-like symptoms before work meetings.
recent_visits: Primary care visit for blood pressure follow-up and medication refills (late January)
Endocrinology lab work review for TSH/T4 dose adjustment (February)
Urgent care visit for chest burning determined to be GERD flare (early February)
Sleep clinic referral intake for snoring/daytime fatigue (scheduled, not yet completed)

finance.debt: Owes about $9,500 on a credit card (car repairs and medical copays) and has a remaining auto loan balance; keeping extra cash on hand in case he needs to hire an employment attorney or has a gap in income if pushed out.
recent_transactions: Paid $165 for thyroid and lipid lab copays at Advocate clinic
Bought a digital voice recorder and encrypted USB drive (~$120) for documenting work issues
Consultation retainer payment to an employment lawyer ($350)
Home Depot purchase for furnace filter packs and PVC fittings (~$78)
Extra payment toward credit card to keep utilization down ($250)

relationships: Partner: Denise R. (long-term girlfriend; supportive but worried about retaliation)
Adult son: Jordan (college graduate; Marek avoids sharing details to not stress him)
Close friend: Luis Navarro (union coworker confidant; meets off-site to talk)
Sister: Tasha Campbell (encourages him to report violations but fears financial fallout)
social.social_media_activity: Low activity; mostly reads neighborhood and trade groups. Has stopped posting about work entirely and tightened privacy settings after noticing coworkers watching his posts.
private_conversations: Discussing suspected refrigerant recovery violations and falsified service logs at work
Talking with Denise about whether to file a complaint with OSHA/IDPH and how to stay anonymous
Confiding in Luis about a supervisor pressuring techs to skip safety steps to hit quotas
Asking his sister for advice on whether to document conversations and how to avoid retaliation

schedule.work_hours: Typically Mon–Fri 7:00am–3:30pm; rotating on-call one week every 6 weeks (evenings/weekends). Overtime spikes during heat waves and cold snaps.
regular_appointments: Blood pressure check-in with primary care every 3–4 months
Quarterly thyroid labs (TSH/T4) and medication review
Monthly union local meeting (first Wednesday evening)
Weekly early-morning pharmacy pickup before shift (as needed)
upcoming_events: Follow-up appointment for sleep clinic evaluation (next month, early morning)
Meeting with employment attorney to review documentation and next steps (Friday after work)
Jordan’s birthday dinner in Logan Square (Saturday evening)
On-call rotation starts in two weeks (weeklong)
schedule.daily_routine: Up around 5:15am, takes levothyroxine before coffee, checks dispatch, drives to first call, eats quick lunches between jobs, tracks BP at home a few nights a week, and tries to wind down early but often stays up organizing notes related to work issues.

professional.company: Mid-sized commercial HVAC contractor serving downtown high-rises and suburban office parks (e.g., "Lakeshore Mechanical Services")
projects: PM contracts for two Loop office buildings (filter changes, belt replacements, coil cleaning)
Rooftop unit troubleshooting and compressor swap at a River North restaurant
Boiler maintenance calls for a small property management portfolio on the North Side
Refrigerant leak checks and recovery documentation for older R-22 systems slated for retrofit
professional.performance_issues: Work quality is solid, but he has become cautious and slower on paperwork because he refuses to sign off on questionable logs; has been excluded from certain high-visibility jobs after raising compliance concerns.
workplace_relationships: Tense dynamic with service manager who emphasizes speed and margin over compliance
One senior tech quietly supports him but avoids being seen together at the shop
Dispatcher treats him neutrally but has started routing him fewer premium overtime calls
Union steward encourages him to document everything and avoid confronting management alone

hobbies: Fishing on Lake Michigan (when schedules allow)
Restoring an older pickup truck in his garage
Watching Cubs games and listening to sports radio
DIY home maintenance and small woodworking projects
habits: Drinks 2–3 cups of coffee most mornings (tries to cut back for blood pressure/GERD)
Keeps a paper notebook and a separate encrypted file of work incidents at home
Checks blood pressure at night a few times per week
Avoids late-night spicy food but slips during stressful weeks
Occasional nicotine gum use to manage stress (doesn’t want coworkers to know)
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a two-bedroom apartment on the Northwest Side of Chicago with Denise; keeps work-related documents in a locked toolbox and is careful about visitors due to the whistleblower situation.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Tries to follow a lower-sodium diet for hypertension and avoids acidic/spicy foods for GERD; eats a lot of quick takeout during busy seasons but aims for grilled chicken, salads, and oatmeal when home.
